The `CoverImage` component uses `ExportedImage` (next-image-export-optimizer), which in turn uses Next.js `Image`. When `fill` is used, Next.js may add a `<link rel="preload">` tag in the `<head>` for images that are "above the fold" (i.e., loaded eagerly without `loading="lazy"`).

The issue is: the Unsplash image URL `https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1466692476868-aef1dfb1e735?auto=format&fit=crop&w=1200&q=80` is in the markdown content of `content/series/markdown-showcase/visuals-and-diagrams.mdx`, but it's appearing as a preloaded resource on the books page.

---
title: "Visuals and Diagrams"
date: "2026-02-13"
category: "Design"
tags: ["visuals", "mermaid"]
---

Visualizing information is key to understanding. Amytis integrates Mermaid.js for diagrams and handles images with ease.

## Mermaid Diagrams

You can write diagrams directly in your markdown:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Idea] --> B{Is it good?}
    B -- Yes --> C[Plant it]
    B -- No --> D[Discard]
    C --> E[Grow]
    E --> F[Evergreen]
```

## Images

Standard markdown images are supported, including relative paths:

![Digital Garden](https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1466692476868-aef1dfb1e735?auto=format&fit=crop&w=1200&q=80)

content.text: The fix is minimal — one attribute added to line 156 of `MarkdownRenderer.tsx`. **What was happening:** - `MarkdownRenderer` is a Server Component (no `'use client'`) - React 19 automatically adds `` to the document `` for `` elements it considers LCP candidates during SSR - The Unsplash image in `visuals-and-diagrams.mdx` is the first (and only) `` on that post page → React 19 treats it as the LCP image and preloads it - When navigating to `/books/` via Next.js client-side routing (SPA), the `` from the previous page's `` isn't cleaned up → Chrome sees it lingering and warns "preloaded but not used" **The fix:** Adding `fetchPriority="low"` to external images in `MarkdownRenderer` tells React 19 these are body-content images (not hero/LCP images), so it skips generating the `` hint for them. Since inline markdown images are always in the content body (never above-the-fold LCP candidates), `low` priority is the correct semantic here.
